title: bug: podman machine init --now flag referenced in docs, not available
body:
The documentation references a `--now` flag, which is not available.

```
$ podman machine init -h
Initialize a virtual machine

Description:
  initialize a virtual machine

Usage:
  podman machine init [options] [NAME]

Examples:
  podman machine init myvm

Options:
      --cpus uint              Number of CPUs (default 1)
      --disk-size uint         Disk size in GB (default 10)
      --ignition-path string   Path to ignition file
      --image-path string      Path to qcow image (default "testing")
  -m, --memory uint            Memory in MB (default 2048)
```

```
$ podman machine init --now
Error: unknown flag: --now
```

```
$ podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec  8 19:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 22:48:10 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/92635c726ac063770f20ce9cc3da48f33a5ad73b/docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-init.1.md?plain=1#L54

comments.body: Will be available in v4.0. I must have backported a patch to v3.4.4 that mentioned it accidentally (we can't backport the flag itself as it's a new feature).
I'm going to close as this is fixed upstream and v4.0 is already in RC stage, final release imminent
ACK, thought that is was something on the way, but wanted to make sure. Thanks for the quick reply
